# Transcrição: 01-What is the Facebook Pixel

- **Fonte:** 01-What is the Facebook Pixel
- **Duração:** 02:38
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:38]

So the Facebook pixel is a piece of code that sits in the HTML of your website and it tracks your analytics on your site So it's not visible So you won't be able to actually see the pixel itself, but it is there and it's tracking what's going on So one really important thing to note is that it doesn't only track activity from people who enter your site from Facebook It tracks all activity regardless of the referral source So it doesn't matter how people came on to your site the pixel is still going to measure the data and the activity that's going on So you'll use the pixel to track people who visit your site and the actions that they carry out while they're on the site This helps you to make sure that your ads are being shown to the right people And the way that this works is by you attaching a piece of code that tells Facebook What a certain action is so you'd put that code on a Web page or on a button Or on an action and then that information gets relayed to Facebook and let's it know what that action is how valuable That is to you and you can therefore try and optimize to get more of that action happening So for example, you could track people clicking on this button or once I click that you could track them landing on this web page Or you could also track conversion events like purchases or leads or people signing up for your newsletter or Registering for a webinar. There's a lot of options in terms of tracking there So you can also build audiences based on the actions that they take on your site and Build lookalike audiences of people similar to people who took desirable actions like making a purchase So you could create an audience of people who added something to their cart But didn't actually purchase it so that you could target those people or you could create an audience of people who have purchased Something and then make a look alike so you can get more people who resemble the people who have purchased an Ecommerce you can also attach a product catalog to your pixel Which is then going to provide Facebook with more data about your products and conversions And then you can use this information to serve more relevant ads to your audience So like I said before you can track people adding things to their cart And then you can actually serve in ads with those specific products that they added into their cart So that's going to make those ads really highly relevant highly targeted and make those people more likely to purchase Then if they'd had seen a generic ad with products that they hadn't shown interest in So the pixels are really valuable tool really important to have that on your website and make sure that it's set up correctly In the next few videos, they're going to cover how to set it up and making sure that it's tracking properly
